title: Ubuntu PPA version of podman has broken dependencies
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Installing `podman` from PPA, now leads to this dependency chain:

```
The following additional packages will be installed:
  containers-common cri-o-1.12 cri-o-runc skopeo-containers
The following NEW packages will be installed:
  containers-common cri-o-1.12 cri-o-runc podman skopeo-containers
0 upgraded, 5 newly installed, 0 to remove and 0 not upgraded.
Need to get 0 B/15.8 MB of archives.
After this operation, 71.4 MB of additional disk space will be used.
Do you want to continue? [Y/n] 
Selecting previously unselected package containers-common.
(Reading database ... 538617 files and directories currently installed.)
Preparing to unpack .../containers-common_0.1.36-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa1_amd64.deb ...
Unpacking containers-common (0.1.36-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa1) ...
Selecting previously unselected package cri-o-runc.
Preparing to unpack .../cri-o-runc_1.0.0-rc5-1~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa69_amd64.deb ...
Unpacking cri-o-runc (1.0.0-rc5-1~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa69) ...
Preparing to unpack .../skopeo-containers_0.1.35-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa2_amd64.deb ...
Unpacking skopeo-containers (0.1.35-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa2) ...
dpkg: error processing archive /var/cache/apt/archives/skopeo-containers_0.1.35-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa2_amd64.deb (--unpack):
 trying to overwrite '/etc/containers/registries.d/default.yaml', which is also in package containers-common 0.1.36-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa1
Selecting previously unselected package cri-o-1.12.
Preparing to unpack .../cri-o-1.12_1.12.9-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa1_amd64.deb ...
Unpacking cri-o-1.12 (1.12.9-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa1) ...
Selecting previously unselected package podman.
Preparing to unpack .../podman_1.0.1-1~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa14_amd64.deb ...
Unpacking podman (1.0.1-1~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa14) ...
Processing triggers for man-db (2.7.5-1) ...
Errors were encountered while processing:
 /var/cache/apt/archives/skopeo-containers_0.1.35-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa2_amd64.deb
E: Sub-process /usr/bin/dpkg returned an error code (1)
```

https://launchpad.net/~projectatomic/+archive/ubuntu/ppa

----

It still has the side-effect of downgrading kubernetes, due to the cri-o dependency.
i.e. podman requires crio-o 1.12 which in turn requires kubernetes 1.12 (see [matrix](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cri-o#compatibility-matrix-cri-o---kubernetes-clusters))

The best solution to this would be to make podman independent of crio (and runc?)
i..e make podman-specific packaging of those, rather than piggy-backing on CRI-O
